coaching.title: Easy Long Run — Hilly
coaching.message: Peak Phase, Week 13: easy Zone 2 run on hilly terrain with ~2,000 ft of vert (calendar has it blocked 5:30–10:00). Keep HR under ~145 bpm and conversational — easy days easy, hard days hard. Power-hike the steepest pitches with hands on quads; that's exactly the Julian Alps movement pattern. Bogotá's 2,600 m altitude is doing extra aerobic work for you — let pace drift slower and hold effort, not splits.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: This is gut-training day: take 60–80g carbs/hr from the first hour, don't wait until you feel it. Pre-run: 80–100g easy carbs 90 min before the 5:30 start. Post-run: 80–100g carbs plus 25–30g protein within 45 minutes, then keep eating — peak-phase days like this need well above the 220g rest-day floor. Hydrate extra for altitude.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Big vert day means eccentric load on the quads — 10 minutes of legs-up-the-wall after your haircut errand, foam roll quads and calves tonight, and protect sleep despite the anniversary dinner: aim to be in bed within 9 hours of finishing. Tomorrow's legs are built tonight.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Phase 4 is the hardest month of the block — 69 days to Kranjska Gora. Today isn't about proving fitness; it's about banking miles cheaply. Practice one race rehearsal cue on the climbs: settle, shorten stride, count 20 steps, repeat. Boring discipline now is what makes the Julian Alps feel familiar in September.
